name: Schoolman Transportation Syst
fleet_size_est: approx 9 vehicles (USDOT) with broader motorcoach/limo fleet under Classic Coach d/b/a
employees_est: approx 30-40 (USDOT 32 drivers; D&B lists 7 at HQ)
districts_or_customers: NYC DOE (Hurricane Sandy displaced-student shuttle contract)
recent_news: Primarily a charter/motorcoach operator d/b/a Classic Coach; historical NYC DOE shuttle work after Hurricane Sandy (litigation in 2016 NY Slip Op 50438(U)). No M&A activity identified.
fit_notes: Borderline fit for Summit: primarily a Long Island charter/motorcoach (Classic Coach) operator, not a core yellow-school-bus district contractor. Long-tenured single-owner (Schoolman family since 1985) suggests succession potential, but limited home-to-school route base reduces tuck-in attractiveness.

name: Holiday Express
employees_est: 8 per filing data; likely more seasonal drivers
districts_or_customers: Grand Island Public Schools (limited busing contract)
recent_news: Family-run multi-generational operator in Central Nebraska; The Independent (2022) profile saluting GIPS bus drivers. No M&A activity identified. Next-gen Brown children (3 of 6) already active in business.
fit_notes: Family-owned since 1961 with three Brown next-gen siblings active in operations, suggesting LOW near-term sale appetite. Small Nebraska footprint with mixed school/charter/limo mix; not a core Summit tuck-in priority unless geographic platform-building in NE is intended.

name: Whitcraft Services Inc Mntnc
recent_news: Long-serving BCPS contractor (since 1932); quoted by Education Week (Aug 2024) on improved driver supply. No M&A activity identified.
fit_notes: Strong Summit tuck-in candidate: 90+ year-old single-owner Baltimore County school bus contractor with clear succession runway under sole owner Ron Prettyman. Stable BCPS contract pipeline; MD is a Summit-relevant state. Fleet size needs diligence.

name: BJS Transportations
districts_or_customers: Greater Chicago area schools, corporate clients, event/casino charters
recent_news: MBE/WBE-certified Chicago South Side operator; no M&A or notable press identified.
fit_notes: Weak Summit fit: predominantly a charter/event/shuttle operator with only ancillary school program work; very small (5 employees) and not a route-based district contractor. Likely a pass for tuck-in unless used for Chicago-area diversity supplier status.

name: Signature School Bus LLC
recent_news: Active FMCSA-registered carrier (USDOT 4269238 / MC 1657735) operating school bus and charter services; one record shows a Richmond, TX dispatch address. No M&A activity identified; little public footprint and no company website.
fit_notes: Possible Houston-area tuck-in but profile is opaque: no website, manager-only listing (O. Khaled), and employee/revenue figures (156 / $10.8M) imply meaningful scale that is not corroborated by public sources. Requires direct outreach/diligence to verify independence, fleet, and district contracts before scoring.
